event: Venetian Lagoon near the Church of Santa Maria della Salute
symbols: church dome rising above the water — the Church as a spiritual landmark anchoring the city
description: A vertical Venetian canal scene showing the grand dome of the Church of Santa Maria della Salute rising above surrounding buildings. Gondolas and small boats glide on the calm blue-green water in the foreground. A stone bridge arches over a smaller canal at the left. Trees and red-roofed buildings nestle around the base of the church. The sky is clear blue with a few white clouds. The palette is luminous with blues, greens, whites, and warm terracotta tones.
theological_description: The Church of Santa Maria della Salute (Our Lady of Health) was built as a votive offering after the devastating plague of 1630-31. The Venetian Senate vowed to build a magnificent church to the Virgin Mary if she would intercede to end the plague. The church's very existence is a theological statement: it testifies to the power of intercessory prayer and the grateful response of a community delivered from catastrophe. Its prominent position at the entrance to the Grand Canal makes it the first sacred landmark visitors encounter, declaring that Venice was saved by grace.

scripture_refs.reason: They cried to the Lord in their trouble and He delivered them — the votive church built in gratitude for deliverance
The Mighty One has done great things for me — Mary's Magnificat, the theological basis for the Marian dedication

_meta.title: Venetian Lagoon near the Church of Santa Maria Della Salute
